overview: The Public Works Committee approved minutes, acknowledged reports, and made several key decisions including awarding a $126,900 recycled asphalt crushing contract to King Gravel, recommending a resolution supporting state transportation funding, directing staff to create enforcement language for sanitary sewer discharge violations with $100/day fines, and approving construction agreements for multiple road projects. The committee also discussed a $9 million grant for Birch Street reconstruction and ongoing road repairs.

agenda.item: Acknowledge April 2026 Water and Sewer Permit
Review 2025 Weston Water Utility PSC Annual Report

discussions.body: Michael reported the village received a Local Road Improvement Grant for Birch Street reconstruction between Schofield Avenue and Community Center Drive, covering over 50% of the estimated $1.5 million project. This 2029 project will likely move to 2028. Michael noted the village has received over $9 million in grant funding over the past 4-5 years. Discussion included Weston Avenue road repairs by Integrity Grading and Excavating for settlement issues over sewer trenches, and upcoming repairs on Schofield Avenue and Fuller Street by contractors HOS Construction and Melvin respectively.

Staff reported on road limit removals coordinated with Marathon County, mailbox and sign repairs, and dewatering operations near Weston Avenue tributary that ran for approximately one week. Discussion addressed flooding issues at Crane Meadows area with groundwater problems, and consideration of permanent pump solutions. Spring street sweeping, hot mix patching, storm damage cleanup on East Nick, and culvert failures on Jones Street were completed. Equipment maintenance and changeover from winter to summer operations was detailed.

Josh reported on water service repair at Caribou Coffee, well maintenance, and meter card maintenance program. Significant discussion focused on clear water infiltration into the sanitary sewer system - April showed 37 million gallons pumped into the system but 53-54 million gallons treated, representing approximately 15 million gallons of clear water. This is attributed to infiltration and residents illegally connecting sump pumps to sanitary sewers. The Kathleen lift station was identified as having clear water discharge from a single residence.

discussions.item: Review 2025 Weston Water Utility PSC Annual Report
discussions.body: Michael presented the annual PSC report showing net operating income of $482,911 for 2025, consistent with the prior year's $490,000. Water loss was 6%. The utility value increased to nearly $34 million, up 6.7% from infrastructure placed in service including wells 7 and 8 and PFAS arrangements. Non-operating revenue included $1.44 million in PFAS settlement funds. The utility serves 6,100 customers total. Motion to acknowledge passed unanimously.

Michael explained this League of Wisconsin Municipalities resolution supports long-term funding solutions for state transportation. The village has decreased public works budgets recently while relying on grants for one-time costs but lacking recurring funding for maintenance and snow plowing. Wisconsin was noted as relatively affordable for automobile ownership compared to neighboring states. Motion to recommend village board adoption passed unanimously.

Michael presented the recycled asphalt crushing project, noting the village's Ryan Street site stockpiles concrete and asphalt that gets crushed into base course. King Gravel submitted the low bid at $4.23/ton for an estimated 30,000 tons totaling $126,900. Analysis showed this saves approximately $11 per cubic yard compared to City of Wausau costs, representing about $130,000 savings across this year's three projects. Motion to award to King Gravel passed unanimously.

Extended discussion on enforcement of illegal sump pump connections to sanitary sewers. Current code allows fines of $100-$1,000 per day per violation. Committee debated appropriate fine levels, with consensus that $100/day is appropriate for first offenses while willful violations should escalate. Josh noted the in-pipe technologies INI study will help identify problem areas. Cost analysis requested for next meeting. Motion to direct staff to create enforcement language with fines not exceeding $100/day passed unanimously.

discussions.item: Water and Sewer Connection Agreement - Radkey Avenue
discussions.body: Michael presented agreement for new duplex at 2230 Radkey Avenue to temporarily connect via long lateral service until Radkey Avenue reconstruction, when owners would pay assessment for main extension. Committee directed that agreement specify reconstruction no later than 2029. Motion to recommend village board authorization passed unanimously.

Agreement with Village of Rothschild for construction on Military Road from Wolfman Street to Helin Street. Weston's share is $94,860 for water main relocation and sanitary sewer work. Steam Construction is the contractor. Motion to recommend village board approval passed unanimously.

Michael presented proposals for construction inspection services. For Alderson/Jelinek roundabout project, MSA proposed $37,315 versus Clark Dietz at $48,910; MSA recommended. For Bladel Avenue, MSA proposed $70,400 versus Clark Dietz at $73,730; Clark Dietz recommended due to having full-time experienced staff rather than summer intern. Motion to approve MSA for roundabout and Clark Dietz for Bladel passed unanimously.

actionItems: Award 2026 recycled asphalt crushing contract to King Gravel at $4.23/ton, estimated $126,900 total
Recommend village board adopt Resolution 2026-014 supporting state transportation funding solutions
Direct staff to create enforcement language for sanitary sewer discharge violations with fines not exceeding $100/day
Recommend village board authorize Radkey Avenue temporary water/sewer connection agreement with reconstruction assessment due no later than 2029
Recommend village board approve Military Road construction agreement with Rothschild for $94,860
Approve MSA for Alderson/Jelinek roundabout construction services and Clark Dietz for Bladel Avenue project
Schedule Bladel Avenue special assessment meeting for Thursday, May 28, 2026 at 6:00 PM
Staff to provide cost analysis of clear water infiltration treatment costs for next meeting
Staff to investigate permanent pump solutions for Weston Avenue/Crane Meadows flooding area
Consider street lighting improvements for Schofield Avenue between Birch Street and Camp Phillips Road in 2030 project
title: Public Works Committee
